user_query:
I’m helping my wife track her pregnancy. Please read these files and generate two outputs:

Inputs to read:
- input/profile.json (contains user_id, role, and LMP)
- input/today.txt (an ISO date to use as the "today" reference for all calculations)
- input/actions.jsonl (events to apply: e.g., mark a standard task complete, add a custom milestone)

What to do:
1) Use the exact date in input/today.txt as "today". Parse the LMP from input/profile.json and calculate gestational age in whole weeks and remaining days using 7-day weeks.
2) Determine the trimester from weeks: <13 first_trimester, <28 second_trimester, else third_trimester.
3) Apply the actions in input/actions.jsonl in order. For this run, you should at least handle:
   - Completing a standard task by its id
   - Adding a custom milestone with a title and target week
4) Build a list of upcoming and overdue standard milestones based on the pregnancy checkup schedule. Only include items that are either upcoming within their reminder window or recently overdue (within 4 weeks of the start week). Do not include items that are far past due or already completed. Also include the newly added custom milestone in the custom list, even if it is not yet upcoming.
5) Create two files:
   - output/status.json — A machine-readable summary with:
     {
       "user_id", "role", "weeks", "days", "trimester",
       "completed_tasks": [standard task ids],
       "custom_milestones": [{"title", "week"}],
       "upcoming_tasks": [{"id", "status"}]  // status: "upcoming" or "overdue"
     }
   - output/partner_summary.md — A short partner-focused summary including the user_id, the role (partner), the gestational age in weeks/days, what’s coming up next, and a clear safety note like "This is not medical advice. Please consult a doctor for concerns."

Notes:
- Keep all paths relative and only use input/ and output/ as above.
- Do not rely on the current system date; only use the date from input/today.txt.
- The standard task ids you may need include: nt_scan, down_screening, anomaly_scan, ogtt, growth_scan, gbs_check, weekly_checkup.
